articleTitle: Bluey the Ride: Here Come the Grannies!
introSection: Bluey the Ride: Here Come the Grannies! is a roller coaster themed after the television series Bluey currently under construction at Alton Towers in Alton, Staffordshire, England, UK.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In January 2025, Alton Towers submitted plans to demolish Postman Pat Parcel Post and replace it with a new children's roller coaster which was rumoured to be themed after Australian children's series Bluey.

Postman Pat Parcel Post soon closed permanently on 2 June 2025, and was demolished to make way for the coaster. In September 2025, it was officially announced by Merlin Entertainments, BBC Studios and Ludo Studio that a Bluey themed Roller Coaster would be coming to Alton Towers.

On 21 January 2026, the coaster's name was revealed as Bluey the Ride: Here Come the Grannies!, being themed to the Bluey episode "Grannies".

On 25 February 2026, the opening date was revealed to be 28 March 2026.

fullText:
Bluey the Ride: Here Come the Grannies! is a roller coaster themed after the television series Bluey currently under construction at Alton Towers in Alton, Staffordshire, England, UK.

In January 2025, Alton Towers submitted plans to demolish Postman Pat Parcel Post and replace it with a new children's roller coaster which was rumoured to be themed after Australian children's series Bluey.

Postman Pat Parcel Post soon closed permanently on 2 June 2025, and was demolished to make way for the coaster. In September 2025, it was officially announced by Merlin Entertainments, BBC Studios and Ludo Studio that a Bluey themed Roller Coaster would be coming to Alton Towers.

On 21 January 2026, the coaster's name was revealed as Bluey the Ride: Here Come the Grannies!, being themed to the Bluey episode "Grannies".

On 25 February 2026, the opening date was revealed to be 28 March 2026.
